design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore interconnected caverns, defeat enemies, collect power-ups that unlock previously inaccessible areas, progressively expand accessible map
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Navigation without map combined with resource scarcity (health, missiles, ammo) and environmental hazards; sequence-breaking creates tension between intended and emergent progression
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lone elite bounty hunter infiltrating alien fortress, acquiring increasingly powerful weaponry and abilities, solving environmental puzzles through gear acquisition
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: No map system forces players to memorize layout and navigate via spatial reasoning; non-linear progression allows sequence breaks via advanced techniques; varied endings reward speedrunning (completion time affects ending); mysterious world with minimal exposition creates sense of discovery; female protagonist in traditionally male action genre

difficulty.continues_system: Password system (cartridge versions) allowing mid-game saves; Famicom Disk System version used battery backup
difficulty.checkpointing: Passwords encode game state; no mid-level checkpoints; password system allows arbitrary save/load at any point
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through sequence breaking; advanced techniques (bomb boost jumps, morph jumps, door glitch, invulnerability exploits, freeze-jump mechanics) allow players to skip intended progression and access endgame areas early; exploration depth creates natural difficulty scaling based on item collection

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up collection system: Morph Ball (mobility), Bombs (tunnel access), Ice Beam (freeze mechanics), Wave Beam, Screw Attack (enemy destruction), Missiles, Varia Suit (heat resistance), Hi-Jump Boots (climbing), Energy Tanks (health expansion), Missile Expansions (ammo expansion). Each power-up gates access to new areas.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Gated by power-up possession and boss defeats. Kraid and Ridley statues must be destroyed to access Tourian (final area). Multiple sequence-break routes bypass intended gating via glitches (door glitch, bomb boost variations, freeze-jump platforms).
replayability_hooks: Speedrunning: varied endings based on completion time incentivize optimization
Sequence breaking: multiple routes to access areas in non-linear order
100% completion: hidden energy tanks and missile expansions scattered throughout world
Glitch exploration: documented advanced techniques enable player mastery progression
Password system enables experimentation without replaying full game

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling: Space Pirate base architecture, alien flora/fauna, technological installations reveal world history
Minimal exposition: brief mission briefing then player left to discover context through exploration
Visual design: distinct biomes (Brinstar, Norfair, Tourian, Kraid's hideout) suggest planetary geology and civilization layers
Enemy design: Space Pirates, aliens, and Metroids reflect hostile ecosystem and pirate occupation
Boss placement: Kraid and Ridley positioned as antagonistic force hierarchy within fortress structure

boss_design.philosophy: Multi-phase encounters requiring adaptation; bosses test acquired weapon arsenals and pattern recognition; defeat gates progression to new areas
notable_bosses: Kraid: large reptilian creature, multi-phase engagement, requires bomb or missile damage, located in Brinstar
Ridley: Space Pirate commander, flying adversary, pattern-based attack telegraphing, located in Norfair
Mother Brain: final biomechanical boss, Tourian location, represents culmination of threat, triggers escape sequence upon defeat

faction_indicators: Galactic Federation: institutional authority, sends lone bounty hunter after retreating from direct assault
Space Pirates: occupying force, technologically advanced, seeking biological weapons supremacy
Samus Aran: lone operative, elite status, operates outside institutional hierarchy, succeeds where military failed
danger_signals: Metroid organisms: existential biological threat, uncontrollable once weaponized, justify extreme countermeasures
Mother Brain: centralized command entity, biomechanical hybrid suggesting technology-biology fusion crisis
Kraid and Ridley: individual power figures within pirate hierarchy, their strength indicates pirate military capacity
environmental_storytelling: Brinstar: organic caverns with technological intrusion (pirate installations), suggests colonization of natural space
Norfair: volcanic hostile terrain, indicates environmental weaponization potential, heat-based progression gating
Tourian: fortress complex architecture, Mother Brain chamber design reflects centralized control structure
Ruins and artifacts: evidence of previous civilization (bird people referenced in instruction manual), replaced by current pirate occupation, cyclic conquest implied
